steps.title: Identify the target meaning
steps.content: The passage describes nature as "gorgeous," with moss "enlivened," pine "resplendent," and festoons of carnival. Mr. Bradford is "conscious" of this vivid visual display. The word must mean an impressive visual sight.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean a striking, impressive visual exhibition. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — spectacle: "this gorgeous spectacle of nature" — spectacle means a visually impressive or striking sight. This perfectly matches the vivid natural scene described. ✓
  • Choice B — reproduction: "this gorgeous reproduction of nature" — reproduction means a copy or duplicate. The passage describes real nature, not a replica. ✗
  • Choice C — pretentiousness: "this gorgeous pretentiousness of nature" — pretentiousness means affectation or trying to seem important. Nature is not described as self-important here. ✗
  • Choice D — disguise: "this gorgeous disguise of nature" — disguise means a concealing costume. Nothing in the passage suggests nature is concealing anything. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
